prompt:
Cron never paged and it cost us a day of retention. nightsweep.ps1 executes the retention planner's sweep.json — archive tasks copy a file into the archive tree, purge tasks delete expired staging files. Last night it printed:

task mon-log: ok
task tue-log: ok
task old-1: ok
task old-2: ok
swept: 4 tasks, 0 failed

and exited 0. Except the tue-log archive never landed in archive/, and old-2's purge target... well, old-2's source was already gone, so 'ok' there is doubly weird. The kicker is the console: there was red error text scrolling past BETWEEN those very 'ok' lines. It complains about a task and then declares it ok in the same breath, the summary says 0 failed, the run exits 0, and nothing ever pages. What I need is boring and strict: a task whose work did not actually happen gets reported 'task <name>: FAILED', the summary counts it, the run exits 65 when anything failed, the tasks after a failure still run, and the errors stop spraying raw red records — the FAILED line IS the report. A fully clean night keeps exiting 0. Fix nightsweep.ps1 and prove it: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_nightsweep.ps1` from the workspace root must pass. The test file is off limits.
notes: 6P5 item 6: ErrorAction misconceptions, PowerShell edition of the bash set-e-misconception family. Cause 1: Copy-Item errors are NON-terminating, so the try/catch around the archive branch never catches without -ErrorAction Stop — the error prints to stderr and $ok stays true (probed 2026-07-16: catch hit False). Cause 2: the purge branch checks $? two statements late — Remove-Item fails, the Add-Content log line succeeds and resets $? to True (probed), so the dead purge reports ok. Both lines look like textbook error handling. Reference fix: -ErrorAction Stop + try/catch in both branches (checking $? immediately is also viable for purge). Tests pin the mixed run byte-exact (FAILED lines, summary count, exit 65, stderr EMPTY — the buggy run fails all four ways), real file effects (archive landed with content, failed archive absent, purge target gone), the clean run (exit 0), and continue-after-failure. Script intentionally leaves $ErrorActionPreference at default — that omission IS the scenario, and the harness law only binds test files. Deterministic; verify run 5x during self-check. Differentiation: bash-relgate/teetrap cover set -e/pipefail; non-terminating-vs-terminating and $?-reset have no bash analog and no existing ps seed touches them.
